england.name: England
england.flagColors: White cross of St George on red background
england.description: England is the largest and most populous country of the United Kingdom. Home to London — one of the world's most global cities — England is famous for its rich history, the monarchy, world-class universities, and cultural landmarks.
facts: England has the world's oldest parliamentary democracy
The Industrial Revolution began in England in the 18th century
England invented the sports of football, cricket, and tennis
The NHS (National Health Service) was founded in England in 1948
English is spoken as a first language by over 400 million people worldwide

landmarks.name: Buckingham Palace
landmarks.desc: The official London residence and administrative headquarters of the monarch of the UK.

landmarks.name: Big Ben & Parliament
landmarks.desc: The iconic clock tower next to the Houses of Parliament in Westminster, London.

landmarks.name: Stonehenge
landmarks.desc: A prehistoric stone monument in Wiltshire, built around 3000 BC. Its purpose remains a mystery.

landmarks.name: Tower of London
landmarks.desc: A historic castle on the north bank of the Thames, used as a royal palace and prison.

landmarks.name: Oxford & Cambridge
landmarks.desc: Two of the world's oldest and most prestigious universities, both founded in the Middle Ages.

scotland.name: Scotland
scotland.flagColors: White diagonal cross (Saltire) on blue background
scotland.description: Scotland occupies the northern third of Great Britain and is known for its dramatic Highland landscapes, ancient castles, whisky distilleries, and rich Celtic heritage. The Scottish people have a distinct culture, language, and legal system.
facts: Scotland has over 790 islands, including the Hebrides, Orkney, and Shetland
Scottish scientist Alexander Fleming discovered penicillin in 1928
Scotland has its own legal and education systems separate from England
The kilt, tartan, and bagpipes are iconic Scottish cultural symbols
Ben Nevis (1,345m) is the highest mountain in the British Isles

landmarks.name: Edinburgh Castle
landmarks.desc: A historic fortress that dominates the skyline of Edinburgh from its position on Castle Rock.

landmarks.name: Loch Ness
landmarks.desc: A large, deep freshwater loch in the Scottish Highlands, famous for the legendary Loch Ness Monster.

landmarks.name: Ben Nevis
landmarks.desc: The highest mountain in the UK at 1,345 metres, located near Fort William in the Highlands.

landmarks.name: Royal Mile
landmarks.desc: The main street of Edinburgh's Old Town, running from Edinburgh Castle to Holyrood Palace.

landmarks.name: Glencoe
landmarks.desc: A stunning glaciated valley in the Scottish Highlands, site of the 1692 Massacre of Glencoe.

wales.name: Wales
wales.flagColors: Red dragon on green and white background
wales.description: Wales is a country of stunning natural landscapes, a living Celtic language, and powerful choral music tradition. The Welsh language (Cymraeg) is spoken by around 29% of the population and all road signs are bilingual. Wales is famous for its castles — it has more castles per square mile than any other country.
facts: Wales has more castles per square mile than any other country in the world
The Welsh language (Cymraeg) is one of Europe's oldest living languages
The world's first passenger railway ran in South Wales in 1807
Rugby union is considered the national sport of Wales
Wales has 3 national parks: Snowdonia, Brecon Beacons, and Pembrokeshire Coast

landmarks.name: Cardiff Castle
landmarks.desc: A medieval castle and Victorian Gothic mansion in the heart of the Welsh capital, Cardiff.

landmarks.name: Snowdonia (Yr Wyddfa)
landmarks.desc: A national park containing Snowdon (1,085m), the highest mountain in Wales and England.

landmarks.name: Caernarfon Castle
landmarks.desc: A magnificent 13th-century castle built by Edward I to control Wales, now a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

landmarks.name: Brecon Beacons
landmarks.desc: A range of mountains and moorland in South Wales, famous for hiking and dark skies.

landmarks.name: Pembrokeshire Coast
landmarks.desc: The only coastal national park in the UK, with dramatic cliffs, sandy beaches, and wildlife.

northern-ireland.name: Northern Ireland
northern-ireland.flagColors: Part of the Union Jack; Ulster Banner features Red Hand of Ulster
northern-ireland.description: Northern Ireland is the smallest of the UK's four nations, situated in the north-east of the island of Ireland. It shares a land border with the Republic of Ireland to the south and west. Northern Ireland is known for its stunning natural scenery, including the famous Giant's Causeway, and its vibrant cities of Belfast and Londonderry/Derry.
facts: The Titanic was built in Belfast's Harland & Wolff shipyard in 1911
Giant's Causeway consists of about 40,000 interlocking basalt columns
Northern Ireland shares a land border with the Republic of Ireland
The city of Derry/Londonderry has a fully intact 17th-century city wall
CS Lewis, author of 'The Chronicles of Narnia', was born in Belfast

landmarks.name: Giant's Causeway
landmarks.desc: An area of about 40,000 interlocking basalt columns on the coast of County Antrim. A UNESCO World Heritage Site.

landmarks.name: Titanic Belfast
landmarks.desc: A world-class visitor attraction built on the site where the RMS Titanic was constructed.

landmarks.name: Carrick-a-Rede Rope Bridge
landmarks.desc: A famous rope bridge near Ballintoy, connecting the mainland to the tiny Carrickarede Island.

landmarks.name: Dark Hedges
landmarks.desc: A stunning avenue of beech trees in County Antrim, famously used in Game of Thrones.

landmarks.name: Belfast City Hall
landmarks.desc: A magnificent Baroque Revival building completed in 1906, the civic building of Belfast.
